claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 8 years old and does not live with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Child does not live with claimant and weekly upkeep (£10.00) is below the Child Benefit rate (£26.05)
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Child does not live with claimant and weekly upkeep (£10.00) is below the Child Benefit rate (£26.05)
circumstances: Child does not live with claimant and weekly upkeep (£10.00) is below the Child Benefit rate (£26.05)
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine whether you're eligible for Child Benefit. To do this properly, I'll need to gather some information about your circumstances and any children you're inquiring about.

Let me start with the first question:

**How many children are you inquiring about for Child Benefit, and what are their names?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! I'm looking to claim Child Benefit. I have one child - my son Alex.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! I have one child to assess: **Alex**.

To determine eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex, I need to gather some details about your circumstances. Let me start with the first question:

**How old is Alex, or what is his date of birth?**

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. That's helpful - Alex is 8 years old.

Now I need to understand your living situation:

**Does Alex live with you, or does he live with someone else?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: He doesn't live with me - he lives with his mum.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
I see. That's important information. Before I can assess your eligibility, I need to understand the arrangement better:

**Are you the father of Alex, and does his mother have parental responsibility for him?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I'm his father, and yes, his mum has parental responsibility for him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for clarifying that. I need to check the specific rules about this situation, so let me read the guidance on Child Benefit eligibility when a child lives with someone else.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. Based on the guidance, I now understand the position better. I have one more important question:

**Are you currently making any financial contributions towards Alex's upkeep (such as towards his food, clothes, accommodation, or other living expenses)? If so, how much are you contributing and how frequently?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I do contribute. I give his mum £10 a week to help out with things like clothes and bits and pieces for him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for that detail. Let me now read the main eligibility criteria to ensure I have all the rules correct before making my final assessment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. I now have the key details I need. Let me assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

Based on the rules, I need to clarify one final point about the amount you're contributing:

**The current Child Benefit rate is £25.60 per week (as of recent rates). You mentioned you contribute £10 per week. To be eligible to claim Child Benefit when your child lives with someone else beyond the initial 8 weeks, you need to be contributing at least the same amount as the Child Benefit payment itself. Are you aware that your contribution of £10 per week is currently less than the Child Benefit rate of £25.60 per week?**

I'm asking to confirm you understand this, as it's important for your eligibility assessment.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Oh, I didn't realize it had to be that much. No, I'm only giving £10 a week - that's what I can manage at the moment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for being honest about that. I now have all the information I need to make a decision about your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
## Summary

Unfortunately, **you are not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex** at this time.

**Here's why:**

Although you are Alex's father and are contributing towards his upkeep, the rules state that when a child lives with someone else, you can only continue to claim Child Benefit beyond the initial 8 weeks if you contribute **at least as much as the Child Benefit payment itself**. The current Child Benefit rate is **£25.60 per week**, but you're contributing £10 per week.

**What you could do:**

1. **Increase your contributions** - If you could increase your payments to at least £25.60 per week, you would become eligible to claim (though you'd need to verify if the 8-week window is still open).

2. **Contact the Child Benefit Office** - They can advise you on your specific situation, especially if the arrangement with Alex's mother has only recently begun.

3. **Check with Alex's mother** - If she's not already claiming Child Benefit for Alex, she may be eligible to do so instead, as she has parental responsibility and Alex lives with her.

I hope this has been helpful. If you have any other questions, feel free to ask!

reasonings: Alex is 8 years old and lives with his mother, not with you. According to the rules, when a child lives with someone else, you can usually get Child Benefit for 8 weeks after the child goes to live with them if nobody else claims. However, it can continue for longer only if you contribute to the child's upkeep by an amount equal to or more than the Child Benefit payment itself. The current Child Benefit rate is £25.60 per week. You are contributing £10 per week, which is less than the required £25.60 per week threshold. Therefore, you are not eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex beyond the initial 8-week period, and it appears the initial 8-week period may have already passed given Alex is now established in his mother's care. You would not be eligible to make a new claim at this stage without meeting the contribution threshold.
arguments.overall_reasoning: You are not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex. While you are his father and contributing to his upkeep, your current contribution of £10 per week falls short of the required £25.60 per week (the current Child Benefit rate) needed to maintain eligibility when a child lives with someone else. To become eligible, you would need to increase your contributions to at least £25.60 per week or more. Alternatively, if Alex's mother is not claiming Child Benefit for him, she may be able to claim it instead. I would recommend contacting the Child Benefit Office to discuss your specific circumstances, particularly if the initial 8-week period has not yet elapsed."
